description: Track down memory leak in long-running Node.js worker that OOMs after 6 hours
system_context: Node.js background worker processing video transcoding jobs. Tech stack: Node 20, TypeScript, BullMQ, Redis, FFmpeg spawned as child_process. Worker OOMs (heap exhausted) after ~6 hours of continuous processing. Restarts drop jobs. 34 files in src/. 76 existing tests. Heap size: 2GB.
turns: Started by reading logs. Worker restarts show 'JavaScript heap out of memory' then 'FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed'. Heap ramps from 180MB on start to 2GB over ~5.5 hours, processing ~340 jobs/hour.
Added --inspect and heap snapshots. node --inspect --max-old-space-size=2048 dist/worker.js. Connected Chrome DevTools. Took heap snapshots at start, +1h, +2h, +3h. Compared.

Heap snapshot diff shows: string objects growing linearly (+4.2MB/hour), ArrayBuffer growing (+18MB/hour), (closure) objects accumulating. The ArrayBuffers all trace back to src/processors/frame-extractor.ts.

Read src/processors/frame-extractor.ts. Uses sharp to extract frames from video. Line 87: creates Sharp instance per frame, doesn't call .destroy(). Line 102: keeps frame buffers in a memo Map indexed by frame number — Map is at module scope, never cleared.

Fixed Sharp instance leak. Wrapped sharp calls in try/finally with instance.destroy(). 12 lines. Fixed the memo Map — moved to per-job scope instead of module scope. Map gets garbage collected when job completes. 23 lines changed.

Re-ran profile. Sharp ArrayBuffers no longer accumulate. But string objects still growing. Deep-dive into retained strings. They're large (avg 2.3kb), contain base64-ish data. Trace: src/logger/transport.ts.

Read src/logger/transport.ts. Custom logger that buffers log lines in memory before flushing to S3 every 60 seconds. Bug at line 54: flush interval was changed to 600 seconds during a prod incident last month and never reverted. Buffer grows unbounded. Fixed to 60s. 1 line change.

Also noticed the logger doesn't cap buffer size. Added hard cap at 10,000 entries — if exceeded, drops oldest entries with a warning log. Prevents unbounded growth even if flush fails. 18 lines. 3 tests.
Re-ran profile. String growth stopped. But (closure) objects still growing. These are heap-retained function closures. Used clinic.js heapprofiler to trace.

Closures trace to src/queue/handlers.ts line 78. Every job registers a setInterval to log progress every 5 seconds. Handler returns on job complete but the interval is never cleared. So every completed job leaves a live interval logging to a dead callback that still holds references to the job's data.
Fix: wrapped in try/finally with clearInterval(intervalId). Verified interval count stable across jobs (used setInterval-no-more lint rule to catch this class of bug in the future).
Re-ran 4-hour load test. Heap grew from 180MB to 220MB over 4 hours (normal GC sawtooth). Then stable. No more OOM.
Ran for 12 hours continuously in staging. Processed 4,128 jobs. Heap peak 287MB. No crashes. No errors. Memory leak fully fixed.
Added regression tests. src/processors/__tests__/frame-extractor.memory.test.ts — runs 1000 iterations, asserts heap growth under 20MB using process.memoryUsage(). 4 tests, one per leak source.
Added CI job memory-test.yml that runs the memory tests in Docker with --max-old-space-size=512. Forces OOM if a regression is introduced. Runs on every PR.
Full test suite: 87/87 passing (76 existing + 11 new memory tests). Deployed to production. Memory gauge in Grafana now shows stable sawtooth pattern vs previous linear ramp-and-crash. Pagers quiet.
